meaning: Living a diligent and productive life that others admire.
context: Living a diligent and productive life that others admire.

usageExample.kr: 오늘부터 6시 기상해서 운동하는 갓생 살 거야.
usageExample.en: I'm going to live a 'God-life' by waking up at 6 and exercising.

meaning: One's form/skill/appearance is insane or at its peak.
context: One's form/skill/appearance is insane or at its peak.
origin: Originally used in sports, now used for anyone doing something impressively.

usageExample.kr: 와, 오늘 너 착장 폼 미쳤다!
usageExample.en: Wow, your outfit today is insane!

meaning: The important thing is an unbreakable heart. Persisting despite failures.
context: The important thing is an unbreakable heart. Persisting despite failures.
origin: Originated from a LoL player's interview during Worlds 2022.

usageExample.kr: 성적이 안 좋지만 중꺾마 정신으로 버틴다.
usageExample.en: My grades are bad, but I'll endure with an unbreakable heart.

meaning: Today's workout finished. Used with workout proof photos.
context: Today's workout finished. Used with workout proof photos.

usageExample.en: I posted a 'workout finished' proof shot on Instagram.

meaning: Seeking a natural encounter to start a romantic relationship.
context: Seeking a natural encounter to start a romantic relationship.

usageExample.en: I prefer natural encounters over blind dates.

meaning: Bought with my own money. Used to show a review is not sponsored.
context: Bought with my own money. Used to show a review is not sponsored.

usageExample.kr: 이건 광고 아니고 진짜 내돈내산 후기야.
usageExample.en: This isn't an ad; it's a review of something I bought with my own money.

meaning: Small but certain happiness found in daily life.
context: Small but certain happiness found in daily life.
origin: Originally from a Haruki Murakami essay.

usageExample.kr: 퇴근 후 맥주 한 잔이 내 소확행이야.
usageExample.en: A glass of beer after work is my small but certain happiness.

meaning: Too Much Information. Sharing details that no one asked for.
context: Too Much Information. Sharing details that no one asked for.

usageExample.kr: 오늘 아침 메뉴는 안 궁금해, 완전 TMI네.
usageExample.en: I'm not curious about your breakfast; that's total TMI.

meaning: The moment of realization after an intense or vain experience (Reality Hit).
context: The moment of realization after an intense or vain experience (Reality Hit).

usageExample.kr: 쇼핑하고 통장 보니까 바로 현타 오네.
usageExample.en: Looking at my bank account after shopping, reality hits me.

meaning: Sudden awkward silence. When the mood suddenly turns cold.
context: Sudden awkward silence. When the mood suddenly turns cold.

usageExample.kr: 부장님 아재 개그 때문에 갑분싸 됐어.
usageExample.en: It became awkward because of the manager's dad joke.

meaning: Insider. Someone who is popular and gets along well with others.
context: Insider. Someone who is popular and gets along well with others.

usageExample.kr: 제 친구는 아는 사람이 정말 많은 인싸예요.
usageExample.en: My friend is an 'insider' who knows so many people.

meaning: Outsider. Someone who prefers to be alone or is not part of the 'cool' group.
context: Outsider. Someone who prefers to be alone or is not part of the 'cool' group.

usageExample.kr: 나는 혼자 노는 게 편한 자발적 아싸야.
usageExample.en: I'm a voluntary 'outsider' who prefers being alone.

meaning: An online broadcast where the host eats a large amount of food.
context: An online broadcast where the host eats a large amount of food.

usageExample.en: Watching a Mukbang before bed makes me hungry.

meaning: Going to a coin karaoke booth alone.
context: Going to a coin karaoke booth alone.

usageExample.en: Going to coin karaoke alone is the best for stress relief.

meaning: You're shortening everything. Used when someone uses too many abbreviations.
context: You're shortening everything. Used when someone uses too many abbreviations.

usageExample.en: You're shortening even that? You really shorten everything.

meaning: Even if I freeze to death, I'll drink iced Americano.
context: Even if I freeze to death, I'll drink iced Americano.

usageExample.en: Even at -10 degrees, I'm an 'iced Americano even if I freeze' member.

meaning: Looking like you didn't put effort into your style, even though you did.
context: Looking like you didn't put effort into your style, even though you did.
origin: Short for 'Ggu-min Deut An Ggu-min Deut'.

usageExample.en: The 'effortless' look is the best for a blind date.

meaning: The most handsome or beautiful member in an idol group.
context: The most handsome or beautiful member in an idol group.

usageExample.en: Our group's visual representative is definitely the center.

meaning: My favorite is my favorite, but Cha Eun-woo is Cha Eun-woo.
context: My favorite is my favorite, but Cha Eun-woo is Cha Eun-woo.
origin: Meaning: No matter who your favorite idol is, everyone agrees Cha Eun-woo is handsome.

usageExample.en: Everyone says 'favorite is favorite but Cha is Cha', he looks amazing in person.

meaning: A disaster brought upon oneself (e.g., overworking yourself).
context: A disaster brought upon oneself (e.g., overworking yourself).

usageExample.kr: 마감 전날까지 놀다니, 이건 완전 스불재야.
usageExample.en: Playing until the day before the deadline, this is a self-inflicted disaster.

meaning: One's own imagination or thoughts presented as if they were official facts.
context: One's own imagination or thoughts presented as if they were official facts.

usageExample.kr: 그건 공식 뉴스가 아니라 네 뇌피셜이지.
usageExample.en: That's not official news; it's just your own theory.

meaning: Very annoying or irritating. 'King' is used as an intensifier.
context: Very annoying or irritating. 'King' is used as an intensifier.

usageExample.kr: 말투 진짜 킹받네, 조용히 좀 해.
usageExample.en: Your way of speaking is so annoying, please be quiet.

meaning: So what? Go watch TV. Used to shut down a conversation annoyingly.
context: So what? Go watch TV. Used to shut down a conversation annoyingly.
origin: Popular among elementary students and Gen Z.

usageExample.en: Whatever I do, so what?

meaning: Lunch menu recommendation.
context: Lunch menu recommendation.

usageExample.en: Can you recommend a lunch menu for today?

meaning: Dinner menu recommendation.
context: Dinner menu recommendation.

usageExample.en: I'm hungry. Dinner recommendation, please!

meaning: The more I see you, the more I dislike you.
context: The more I see you, the more I dislike you.

usageExample.en: I liked them at first, but I'm disappointed because they're the type that gets worse as I see them.

meaning: Lost, but fought well.
context: Lost, but fought well.

usageExample.kr: 우리 팀 오늘 비록 졌지만 졌잘싸였어!
usageExample.en: Our team lost today, but we fought well!

meaning: Copy and paste. Used for repeated text or people who look exactly alike.
context: Copy and paste. Used for repeated text or people who look exactly alike.

usageExample.en: This report is just at the level of copy-paste.

meaning: Gathering everything, including one's soul, to achieve something (usually buying a house).
context: Gathering everything, including one's soul, to achieve something (usually buying a house).

usageExample.kr: 영끌해서 아파트 샀는데 대출 이자가 무서워.
usageExample.en: I gathered everything to buy an apartment, but I'm scared of the loan interest.

meaning: Fact violence. Attacking someone with painful, undeniable truths.
context: Fact violence. Attacking someone with painful, undeniable truths.

usageExample.kr: 친구가 나한테 팩폭 해서 정신이 번쩍 들었어.
usageExample.en: My friend hit me with the truth, and I woke up.

meaning: Work-Life Balance.
context: Work-Life Balance.

usageExample.en: To me, work-life balance is more important than money.

meaning: Small but certain embezzlement. Small 'revenges' like taking office supplies home.
context: Small but certain embezzlement. Small 'revenges' like taking office supplies home.

usageExample.kr: 회사 탕비실 믹스커피 챙기는 게 내 소확횡이야.
usageExample.en: Taking mix coffee from the office pantry is my 'small embezzlement'.

meaning: To copy someone's style or items. Named after a character in 'Cheese in the Trap'.
context: To copy someone's style or items. Named after a character in 'Cheese in the Trap'.
origin: Used when someone buys exactly what someone else has.

usageExample.kr: 블랙핑크 제니 옷 손민수 하고 싶어.
usageExample.en: I want to copy Jennie's outfit.

meaning: Person by person. Depends on the individual.
context: Person by person. Depends on the individual.

usageExample.kr: 그 회사는 분위기가 어때? - 사바사지 뭐.
usageExample.en: How's the atmosphere at that company? - It depends on the person.

meaning: Case by case. Depends on the situation.
context: Case by case. Depends on the situation.

usageExample.en: When will the results come out? - That depends on the case.

meaning: I have a lot to say, but I won't.
context: I have a lot to say, but I won't.

usageExample.en: That behavior just now was... I'll just say nothing.

meaning: The sudden realization of an empty bank account.
context: The sudden realization of an empty bank account.
origin: Short for 'Gap-ja-gi Tong-jang-eul bo-ni Al-ba-ha-go sip-da'.

usageExample.kr: 옷 사고 나서 갑통알 와서 알바 구하는 중이야.
usageExample.en: After buying clothes, I realized I'm broke and am looking for a part-time job.

meaning: Knowing when to join in and when to leave.
context: Knowing when to join in and when to leave.

usageExample.kr: 그 자리는 네가 갈 자리가 아니야. 낄낄빠빠 좀 해.
usageExample.en: That's not a place for you. Know when to stay out.

meaning: You have already decided on the answer, and I just have to say it.
context: You have already decided on the answer, and I just have to say it.

usageExample.kr: 나 오늘 예뻐? - 어휴, 답정너 대답 듣고 싶어서 그러지?
usageExample.en: Am I pretty today? - Sigh, you're just looking for the answer you want, right?

meaning: Didn't ask and not curious.
context: Didn't ask and not curious.

usageExample.en: Guess what I did yesterday... - Didn't ask, don't care!

meaning: The perfect son of one's mother's friend (talented, handsome, successful).
context: The perfect son of one's mother's friend (talented, handsome, successful).

usageExample.kr: 걔는 공부도 잘하고 성격도 좋아서 완전 엄친아잖아.
usageExample.en: He's smart and has a great personality; he's the perfect 'mother's friend's son'.

meaning: Chemistry between people.
context: Chemistry between people.

usageExample.kr: 두 주인공의 케미가 영화의 흥행 비결이야.
usageExample.en: The chemistry between the two leads is the secret to the movie's success.

meaning: The stage before a romantic relationship (flirting).
context: The stage before a romantic relationship (flirting).

usageExample.kr: 우리 지금 썸 타는 사이인 것 같아.
usageExample.en: I think we're in the 'something' stage.

meaning: Push and pull in a relationship.
context: Push and pull in a relationship.

usageExample.en: If you only play push and pull, the other person gets tired.

meaning: More attractive the more you look at them.
context: More attractive the more you look at them.

usageExample.kr: 그 사람은 처음보다 볼수록 볼매 스타일이야.
usageExample.en: That person is more charming the more I see them.

meaning: Extreme disgust.
context: Extreme disgust.

usageExample.kr: 청소를 안 하는 건 정말 극혐이야.
usageExample.en: Not cleaning is absolutely disgusting.

meaning: Getting one's feelings hurt.
context: Getting one's feelings hurt.

usageExample.kr: 네 무관심 때문에 나 마상 입었어.
usageExample.en: I got hurt because of your indifference.

meaning: Have a delicious lunch.
context: Have a delicious lunch.

usageExample.en: Did everyone have a delicious lunch?

meaning: To be honest.
context: To be honest.

usageExample.kr: 솔까말 이번 계획은 성공하기 힘들 것 같아.
usageExample.en: To be honest, I think this plan will be hard to succeed.

meaning: Eye-catching.
context: Eye-catching.

usageExample.en: Your outfit today is totally eye-catching!

meaning: Unexpectedly falling in love with a celebrity, like a sudden 'car accident' of fandom.
context: Unexpectedly falling in love with a celebrity, like a sudden 'car accident' of fandom.

usageExample.kr: 어제 드라마 보고 남주한테 덕통사고 당했어.
usageExample.en: I fell in love with the lead actor like a traffic accident after watching the drama.

meaning: Hiding one's fan identity in public to act like a 'normal' person.
context: Hiding one's fan identity in public to act like a 'normal' person.

usageExample.kr: 회사에서는 일코 중이라 책상에 굿즈 안 둬.
usageExample.en: I'm doing Il-ko at work, so I don't keep goods on my desk.

meaning: Since you're going to be a fan anyway, let's be a happy fan.
context: Since you're going to be a fan anyway, let's be a happy fan.

usageExample.en: Don't get stressed, let's be happy fans!

meaning: The moment or process of becoming a fan of a certain celebrity or field.
context: The moment or process of becoming a fan of a certain celebrity or field.

usageExample.en: I became a fan right after hearing this song.

meaning: One's absolute favorite member in a group or a favorite character.
context: One's absolute favorite member in a group or a favorite character.

usageExample.kr: 내 최애는 이번에 센터 맡은 멤버야.
usageExample.en: My favorite is the member who took the center position this time.

meaning: One's second favorite member or character.
context: One's second favorite member or character.

usageExample.en: My favorite is Jimin, and my second favorite is Jungkook.

meaning: A fan who runs a high-quality photography website for a celebrity.
context: A fan who runs a high-quality photography website for a celebrity.

usageExample.kr: 오늘 콘서트에 홈마들 진짜 많이 왔더라.
usageExample.en: A lot of Home Masters came to the concert today.

meaning: One's golden age or the peak of their beauty/career.
context: One's golden age or the peak of their beauty/career.
origin: Originated from football fans discussing Alan Smith's 'Leeds United' days.

usageExample.kr: 저 가수는 지금이 완전 리즈 시절이야.
usageExample.en: That singer is in their golden age right now.

meaning: The member who gets a close-up shot at the end of a performance and looks particularly beautiful.
context: The member who gets a close-up shot at the end of a performance and looks particularly beautiful.

usageExample.en: Who was the ending fairy of today's stage?

meaning: When an old song or content suddenly climbs up the charts long after its release.
context: When an old song or content suddenly climbs up the charts long after its release.
origin: Opposite of 'Jeong-ju-haeng' (staying on top or watching in order).

usageExample.en: Brave Girls' Rollin' is a legend of climbing back up the charts.

meaning: Watching a whole series or webtoon from the first episode to the last.
context: Watching a whole series or webtoon from the first episode to the last.
origin: Originally meant running in the right direction.

usageExample.en: I binge-watched the drama from episode 1 over the weekend.

meaning: Spoiler. Revealing important plot points of a movie or drama.
context: Spoiler. Revealing important plot points of a movie or drama.

usageExample.en: Don't spoil it because I'm going to watch the movie!

meaning: Perfectly synchronized dancing where every movement is sharp like a knife.
context: Perfectly synchronized dancing where every movement is sharp like a knife.
origin: Kal' means knife, 'Gun-mu' means group dance.

usageExample.en: This group is famous for their razor-sharp synchronized dance.

meaning: The member responsible for being the 'face' or most handsome/beautiful in a group.
context: The member responsible for being the 'face' or most handsome/beautiful in a group.

usageExample.en: Who is the visual representative in the team?

meaning: Someone with a uniquely beautiful or attractive voice that 'beats up' others (in a good way).
context: Someone with a uniquely beautiful or attractive voice that 'beats up' others (in a good way).
origin: Eum-saek' means vocal color, 'Kkang-pae' means gangster.

usageExample.en: IU really has a killer vocal color.

meaning: Extremely difficult ticketing that is as fierce as a bloodbath.
context: Extremely difficult ticketing that is as fierce as a bloodbath.

usageExample.en: Booking tickets for Lim Young-woong's concert was a total bloodbath.

meaning: A cringey older man who thinks he's a romantic but is actually quite annoying.
context: A cringey older man who thinks he's a romantic but is actually quite annoying.
origin: Short for 'Yeon-ae Seot-eun Bok-hak-saeng' (Returnee student clumsy at dating).

usageExample.en: That guy talks just like a cringey returnee student.

meaning: A fan who successfully meets their idol or works with them.
context: A fan who successfully meets their idol or works with them.

usageExample.kr: 팬이었던 배우랑 같이 연기하다니, 완전 성덕이네!
usageExample.en: Acting with the actor you were a fan of? You're a total successful fan!

meaning: Fan signing event where fans can meet and get autographs from celebrities.
context: Fan signing event where fans can meet and get autographs from celebrities.

usageExample.en: I hope I win the lottery for this fan signing event.

meaning: Merchandise related to a celebrity, anime, or brand.
context: Merchandise related to a celebrity, anime, or brand.

usageExample.kr: 이번 굿즈 디자인 진짜 잘 뽑혔다.
usageExample.en: The design for this merchandise came out really well.

meaning: The period when someone denies they have become a fan, even though they clearly are.
context: The period when someone denies they have become a fan, even though they clearly are.
origin: Usually involves watching all videos of the celebrity while saying 'I'm not a fan'.

usageExample.kr: 나 입덕 부정기 끝났어. 이제 팬이야.
usageExample.en: My denial period is over. I'm a fan now.

meaning: When a singer or group releases a new album and starts promotions again.
context: When a singer or group releases a new album and starts promotions again.

usageExample.en: My favorite group is making a comeback next month!

meaning: The first public appearance or official start of a celebrity's career.
context: The first public appearance or official start of a celebrity's career.

usageExample.en: When is this trainee going to debut?

meaning: A fan-cam; a video of a specific celebrity filmed by a fan.
context: A fan-cam; a video of a specific celebrity filmed by a fan.
origin: Short for 'Jik-jeop jjigeun camera' (Directly filmed camera).

usageExample.kr: 하니 레전드 직캠 못 본 사람 있어?
usageExample.en: Is there anyone who hasn't seen Hani's legendary fan-cam?

meaning: The member who stands in the middle of a group formation during a performance.
context: The member who stands in the middle of a group formation during a performance.
origin: Often the most popular or visual-focused member.

usageExample.en: Who is the center for this song?

meaning: An employee who gets paid but does little to no work, 'stealing' their salary.
context: An employee who gets paid but does little to no work, 'stealing' their salary.

usageExample.en: I totally slacked off and 'stole' my salary at work today.

meaning: An older person or boss who forces their outdated ideas on younger people.
context: An older person or boss who forces their outdated ideas on younger people.
origin: Originally slang for teachers, now used for anyone acting authoritative based on age.

usageExample.en: Our manager is a typical 'kkon-dae'.

meaning: Satisfaction relative to the price. Spending money on something that makes you mentally happy regardless of cost.
context: Satisfaction relative to the price. Spending money on something that makes you mentally happy regardless of cost.

usageExample.en: It's expensive, but the design is so pretty, so it's worth it for my satisfaction.

meaning: Being so eye-catching that it 'steals' everyone's attention.
context: Being so eye-catching that it 'steals' everyone's attention.

usageExample.en: Your red hair is really stealing the show.

meaning: To be honest; frankly speaking.
context: To be honest; frankly speaking.

usageExample.en: To be honest, this plan isn't that great.

meaning: Brunch; a meal between breakfast and lunch.
context: Brunch; a meal between breakfast and lunch.

usageExample.kr: 일요일엔 늦잠 자고 아점 먹는 게 최고지.
usageExample.en: Sleeping in and having brunch on Sundays is the best.

meaning: Eating alone.
context: Eating alone.

usageExample.kr: 요즘은 식당에서 혼밥 하는 게 유행이야.
usageExample.en: Eating alone at restaurants is a trend these days.

meaning: Drinking alcohol alone.
context: Drinking alcohol alone.

usageExample.kr: 퇴근하고 집에서 혼술 하는 게 낙이야.
usageExample.en: My joy is drinking alone at home after work.

meaning: Cost-effectiveness; getting good quality for a low price.
context: Cost-effectiveness; getting good quality for a low price.

usageExample.en: This restaurant is really cost-effective.

meaning: The fun of spending all your small change or small amounts of money on small luxuries.
context: The fun of spending all your small change or small amounts of money on small luxuries.

usageExample.en: I had so much fun spending money at Daiso today.

meaning: Something so cool or pretty that you feel you absolutely must own it.
context: Something so cool or pretty that you feel you absolutely must own it.
origin: Gak' is used to express a likely situation or necessity.

usageExample.en: These shoes are definitely worth owning.

meaning: Have a delicious lunch.
context: Have a delicious lunch.

usageExample.en: Everyone, have a delicious lunch!

meaning: Getting one's feelings hurt; emotional trauma.
context: Getting one's feelings hurt; emotional trauma.

usageExample.en: If you say it like that, I get hurt.

meaning: Chemistry between people; how well they get along.
context: Chemistry between people; how well they get along.

usageExample.en: The chemistry between the two leads is insane.

meaning: The 'something' between two people before they start dating; flirting.
context: The 'something' between two people before they start dating; flirting.

usageExample.kr: 너희 둘 요즘 썸 타는 거 아니야?
usageExample.en: Aren't you two flirting these days?

meaning: Push and pull in a romantic relationship (playing hard to get).
context: Push and pull in a romantic relationship (playing hard to get).

usageExample.en: Push and pull is essential when dating.

meaning: Someone who gets more attractive the more you look at them.
context: Someone who gets more attractive the more you look at them.

usageExample.en: That person gets more charming the more you see them.

meaning: The perfect daughter of one's mother's friend.
context: The perfect daughter of one's mother's friend.

usageExample.en: My cousin is the perfect 'mother's friend's daughter'.

meaning: I have to go to work again tomorrow.
context: I have to go to work again tomorrow.

usageExample.kr: 일요일 밤엔 항상 내또출 때문에 우울해.
usageExample.en: I'm always depressed on Sunday nights because I have to work again tomorrow.

meaning: Annual leave; paid time off.
context: Annual leave; paid time off.

usageExample.kr: 내일 연차 써서 회사 안 가!
usageExample.en: I'm using my annual leave tomorrow, so I'm not going to work!

meaning: Overtime work; working late into the night.
context: Overtime work; working late into the night.
origin: A common but dreaded part of Korean office culture.

usageExample.en: Overtime is confirmed for today again.

meaning: Resigning from a job.
context: Resigning from a job.
origin: Often called the 'dream' of many office workers.

usageExample.en: I finally quit my job!

meaning: A company dinner or get-together after work.
context: A company dinner or get-together after work.
origin: Sometimes seen as an extension of work, but changing lately.

usageExample.en: A company dinner was suddenly scheduled for today.

meaning: Getting someone's phone number, usually for dating purposes.
context: Getting someone's phone number, usually for dating purposes.

usageExample.en: Someone asked for my number on the street.

meaning: Extreme disgust or hatred toward something.
context: Extreme disgust or hatred toward something.

usageExample.en: I absolutely hate bugs.

meaning: No answer/solution. Used when a situation or a person is hopeless.
context: No answer/solution. Used when a situation or a person is hopeless.
origin: Combines 'No' and 'Dap' (Answer). Originated from gaming where a player's strategy is unsalvageable.

usageExample.en: This game round is seriously hopeless.

meaning: To lead a team to victory or play a decisive role in success.
context: To lead a team to victory or play a decisive role in success.
origin: Loanword from English 'Carry' in games like LoL.

usageExample.en: You totally carried this project!

meaning: To be carried by a strong player without doing much work oneself.
context: To be carried by a strong player without doing much work oneself.
origin: Metaphor for 'riding a bus' driven by someone else. Often used in group projects too.

usageExample.kr: 이번 과제는 조장 덕분에 버스 탔다.
usageExample.en: I got a free ride on this assignment thanks to the team leader.

meaning: A player who intentionally ruins the game for their own team.
context: A player who intentionally ruins the game for their own team.

usageExample.kr: 제발 트롤 짓 좀 하지 마.
usageExample.en: Please stop trolling.

meaning: Playing a game very seriously and intensely.
context: Playing a game very seriously and intensely.

usageExample.en: Stop joking around, let's play seriously now.

meaning: Playing a game for fun rather than focusing solely on winning.
context: Playing a game for fun rather than focusing solely on winning.

usageExample.en: Our goal is to have fun, not just win.

meaning: The maximum level attainable in a game. Also used for masters in real life.
context: The maximum level attainable in a game. Also used for masters in real life.

usageExample.en: Their cooking skills are at the maximum level.

meaning: A beginner or newcomer in a game or field.
context: A beginner or newcomer in a game or field.

usageExample.en: I'm still a newbie, so please be kind.

meaning: A highly experienced veteran who has played for a long time (lit. 'stagnant water').
context: A highly experienced veteran who has played for a long time (lit. 'stagnant water').
origin: Initially derogatory, but now often used to describe someone with god-like expertise.

usageExample.en: This game is full of veterans only.

meaning: An even more extreme version of 'Goin-mul' (lit. 'rotten water').
context: An even more extreme version of 'Goin-mul' (lit. 'rotten water').
origin: Describes someone whose skill level is beyond imagination.

usageExample.en: That person's skill is at a 'rotten water' level.

meaning: A masterpiece or an exceptionally great game.
context: A masterpiece or an exceptionally great game.

usageExample.en: Zelda is a legendary 'God-game'.

meaning: A ruined or poor-quality game.
context: A ruined or poor-quality game.

usageExample.en: It became a ruined game after one update.

meaning: Spending real money to buy in-game items or currency.
context: Spending real money to buy in-game items or currency.

usageExample.en: I spent some real cash to buy items.

meaning: Doing something repeatedly until it becomes annoying or boring.
context: Doing something repeatedly until it becomes annoying or boring.
origin: Originally from Naruto (Chidori), but the meme shifted to meaning 'overdoing it'.

usageExample.en: Stop at the first joke, don't overdo it.

meaning: Attention-seeking behavior or provoking others to get a reaction.
context: Attention-seeking behavior or provoking others to get a reaction.

usageExample.en: He's being an attention seeker to get noticed.

meaning: Crushing a difficult boss/enemy with overwhelming damage alone.
context: Crushing a difficult boss/enemy with overwhelming damage alone.

usageExample.en: I cleared it with raw damage even without knowing the strategy.

meaning: A universal or standard rule that everyone follows (lit. 'National Rule').
context: A universal or standard rule that everyone follows (lit. 'National Rule').
origin: Used for common sense or unspoken agreements in daily life.

usageExample.en: Pickled radish with chicken is the national rule.

meaning: To obtain a rare or valuable item.
context: To obtain a rare or valuable item.

usageExample.en: I got a great find at the sale yesterday!

meaning: The highlight or 'killing point' of a video, song, or story.
context: The highlight or 'killing point' of a video, song, or story.

usageExample.en: The highlight of this video is at the 30-second mark.

meaning: An unexpected or indirect 'loss' when compared to someone else.
context: An unexpected or indirect 'loss' when compared to someone else.
origin: Used when someone else's success makes you look bad relatively.

usageExample.en: I suffered an unexpected loss at my friend's wedding news.

meaning: Mental breakdown; being shocked or confused.
context: Mental breakdown; being shocked or confused.

usageExample.en: I had a mental breakdown after seeing my test score.

meaning: Delivering maximum damage or a sharp verbal attack.
context: Delivering maximum damage or a sharp verbal attack.

usageExample.en: I attacked my friend with harsh facts.

meaning: A real-life physical fight between people who met online.
context: A real-life physical fight between people who met online.

usageExample.en: We almost had a real-life fight after arguing in the comments.

meaning: Incredible, impressive, or great (often used for skill or quantity).
context: Incredible, impressive, or great (often used for skill or quantity).
origin: Originally a dialect, now common internet slang.

usageExample.en: Today's lunch menu is incredible.

meaning: To be extremely impressed or scared (lit. 'to pee one's pants').
context: To be extremely impressed or scared (lit. 'to pee one's pants').

usageExample.kr: 방금 그 춤 실력 진짜 지렸다.
usageExample.en: That dance skill just now was mind-blowing.

meaning: Legitimacy or having a solid foundation. 'The OG'.
context: Legitimacy or having a solid foundation. 'The OG'.
origin: Used to praise something as authentic or high-quality.

usageExample.en: This restaurant's tonkatsu is the real OG.

meaning: Used as a prefix to emphasize that something is the absolute best.
context: Used as a prefix to emphasize that something is the absolute best.

usageExample.en: Son Heung-min is truly the 'King-God-General'.

meaning: Used to describe someone who is virtuous, talented, or great (lit. 'Light').
context: Used to describe someone who is virtuous, talented, or great (lit. 'Light').
origin: Placed before a name, like 'Bit-Heung-Min'. Equivalent to GOAT.

usageExample.en: Yoo Jae-suk... he is just light.

meaning: Cutting ties with someone or stopping an investment to prevent further loss.
context: Cutting ties with someone or stopping an investment to prevent further loss.
origin: Originally a stock market term, now used for friendships.

usageExample.kr: 그런 친구는 빨리 손절하는 게 좋아.
usageExample.en: It's better to cut ties with that kind of friend quickly.

meaning: Closing a position to realize profit. Finishing while ahead.
context: Closing a position to realize profit. Finishing while ahead.

usageExample.en: I made a decent profit and finished.

meaning: A sudden and huge surge in price, popularity, or value.
context: A sudden and huge surge in price, popularity, or value.
origin: Commonly used in stocks, crypto, and YouTube views.

usageExample.kr: 어제 올린 영상이 자고 일어나니 떡상했어!
usageExample.en: The video I posted yesterday went viral overnight!

meaning: A sudden and huge crash in price or value.
context: A sudden and huge crash in price or value.

usageExample.en: My crypto returns are crashing.

meaning: Holding on for dear life; persisting through a tough situation.
context: Holding on for dear life; persisting through a tough situation.
origin: Short for 'Jon-na Beo-ti-gi' (Enduring like crazy). Popularized by crypto investors.

usageExample.en: I'm holding on until I make a profit.

meaning: Fabricating a story or faking a situation to trick people.
context: Fabricating a story or faking a situation to trick people.
origin: Used to call out fake 'viral' posts or staged videos.

usageExample.kr: 이거 주작 아니야? 너무 말이 안 돼.
usageExample.en: Isn't this staged? It makes no sense.

meaning: Great quality or quantity for the price; excellent value.
context: Great quality or quantity for the price; excellent value.
origin: Derived from a high-quality convenience store lunchbox by Kim Hye-ja.

usageExample.kr: 이 식당은 양도 많고 진짜 혜자다.
usageExample.en: This restaurant is generous and a great value.

meaning: Poor quality or quantity for a high price; bad value.
context: Poor quality or quantity for a high price; bad value.
origin: Derived from a poor-quality food brand by Kim Chang-ryeol.

usageExample.kr: 가격에 비해 너무 부실해, 완전 창렬이야.
usageExample.en: It's so poor for the price, a total rip-off.

meaning: Something disappearing instantly (lit. 'instant deletion').
context: Something disappearing instantly (lit. 'instant deletion').

usageExample.en: The movie was so good, 2 hours flew by instantly.

meaning: Didn't ask and not curious.
context: Didn't ask and not curious.

usageExample.en: That's your business, I didn't ask and I'm not curious!

meaning: You have already decided on the answer, and I just have to say it.
context: You have already decided on the answer, and I just have to say it.

usageExample.en: You're acting like you already have the answer you want to hear.

meaning: Indisputable; a situation where you are stuck and can't deny it.
context: Indisputable; a situation where you are stuck and can't deny it.

usageExample.en: The evidence is so clear, it's indisputable.

meaning: I have a lot to say, but I won't.
context: I have a lot to say, but I won't.

usageExample.en: It was so ridiculous today... I'll just say nothing.

meaning: The sudden realization of an empty bank account.
context: The sudden realization of an empty bank account.
origin: Short for 'Gap-ja-gi Tong-jang-eul bo-ni Al-ba-ha-go sip-da' (Suddenly seeing my balance, I want to work part-time).

usageExample.en: Bought a luxury item and immediately felt my empty bank account.

meaning: Knowing when to join in and when to leave a conversation/situation.
context: Knowing when to join in and when to leave a conversation/situation.

usageExample.kr: 낄낄빠빠 좀 잘해라, 지금은 올 때가 아니야.
usageExample.en: Know your place, now is not the time to show up.

meaning: Let's live comfortably in this complex world.
context: Let's live comfortably in this complex world.

usageExample.kr: 남의 눈치 보지 말고 복세편살 하자!
usageExample.en: Let's live comfortably and not care what others think!

meaning: Sorry I couldn't protect you.
context: Sorry I couldn't protect you.
origin: Short for 'Ji-kyeo-ju-ji Mot-hae Mi-an-hae'. Often used for funny/bad photos of friends.

usageExample.en: Sorry the photo is so funny, I couldn't protect you!

meaning: Pity or a situation so sad it makes your eyes damp.
context: Pity or a situation so sad it makes your eyes damp.
origin: Short for 'An-gu-e Seup-gi-ga cha-da' (Dampness filling the eyeballs). A bit old school but still used.

usageExample.en: It's so sad that your plan failed.

meaning: Completely precious; very valuable.
context: Completely precious; very valuable.

usageExample.en: This bag is my completely precious item.

meaning: Studying hard.
context: Studying hard.

usageExample.en: It's exam period, I gotta study hard!

meaning: An outburst of anger driven by an inferiority complex.
context: An outburst of anger driven by an inferiority complex.

usageExample.kr: 걔는 잘나가는 사람만 보면 열폭 하더라.
usageExample.en: He has an outburst whenever he sees someone successful.

meaning: Keeping something for personal use/collection.
context: Keeping something for personal use/collection.

usageExample.kr: 이 사진은 너무 예뻐서 갠소할 거야.
usageExample.en: This photo is so pretty, I'm going to keep it for myself.

meaning: Literally 'Actually, it's better.' Used to put a positive spin on a bad or unexpected situation.
context: Literally 'Actually, it's better.' Used to put a positive spin on a bad or unexpected situation.
origin: Popularized by streamers and Gen Z as a way to maintain a positive mindset.

usageExample.kr: 기차 놓쳤는데 덕분에 맛집 찾았네. 오히려 좋아!
usageExample.en: I missed the train but found a great restaurant. Actually, it's better!

meaning: Waking up very early to do productive activities like reading or exercising before the day starts.
context: Waking up very early to do productive activities like reading or exercising before the day starts.
origin: A global trend that became a massive self-development movement among Korean Gen Z.

usageExample.en: I'm challenging myself to a Miracle Morning starting tomorrow!

meaning: Short for 'Ask me anything.' Frequently used on Instagram Stories' Q&A feature.
context: Short for 'Ask me anything.' Frequently used on Instagram Stories' Q&A feature.

usageExample.en: I'll do an AMA on Instagram tonight!

meaning: A male friend who is 'just a friend' (not a boyfriend).
context: A male friend who is 'just a friend' (not a boyfriend).

usageExample.en: He's just an old male friend.

meaning: A female friend who is 'just a friend' (not a girlfriend).
context: A female friend who is 'just a friend' (not a girlfriend).

usageExample.en: What's wrong with watching a movie with a female friend?

meaning: Seeking artificial encounters (e.g., dating apps or blind dates).
context: Seeking artificial encounters (e.g., dating apps or blind dates).

usageExample.kr: 나는 자만추보다 인만추가 더 효율적인 것 같아.
usageExample.en: I think artificial encounters are more efficient than natural ones.

meaning: A guy who is nerdy/geeky but in a very charming and handsome way.
context: A guy who is nerdy/geeky but in a very charming and handsome way.
origin: A popular romantic trope in Korean dramas and webtoons.

usageExample.kr: 안경 쓴 모습이 완전 너드남 정석이네.
usageExample.en: The way he looks in glasses is the textbook definition of a charming nerd guy.

meaning: Godly perfect. Used when someone or something is flawless.
context: Godly perfect. Used when someone or something is flawless.

usageExample.en: The visuals of this stage are truly godly perfect.

meaning: A cute way to say 'dog' (puppy).
context: A cute way to say 'dog' (puppy).
origin: Based on 'Id-eo-che' where the word '멍멍이' (meong-meong-i) looks similar to '댕댕이'.

usageExample.en: Isn't my puppy so cute?

meaning: Cute. A visual pun on the word '귀엽다' (Gwi-yeop-da).
context: Cute. A visual pun on the word '귀엽다' (Gwi-yeop-da).
origin: Another example of 'Id-eo-che' popular in community sites like DC Inside.

usageExample.en: This cat photo is really cute.

meaning: A masterpiece song. A visual pun on '명곡' (Myeong-gok).
context: A masterpiece song. A visual pun on '명곡' (Myeong-gok).
origin: The character '명' looks like '띵' in certain fonts.

usageExample.en: This song is a masterpiece of my life.

meaning: Tears. The word '눈물' (nun-mul) turned upside down looks like '롬곡'.
context: Tears. The word '눈물' (nun-mul) turned upside down looks like '롬곡'.
origin: Used when one is 'crying' (either sadly or happily).

usageExample.en: It's so touching, I'm crying... [upside down]

meaning: Feeling awkward or embarrassed.
context: Feeling awkward or embarrassed.

usageExample.en: I said it not knowing it was a secret... awkward.

meaning: Totally my style.
context: Totally my style.

usageExample.en: The vibe of this cafe is totally my style.

meaning: Forced tension or energy; faking excitement.
context: Forced tension or energy; faking excitement.

usageExample.kr: 리액션이 너무 억텐인 게 티 나.
usageExample.en: It's obvious that your reaction is forced.

meaning: Real/genuine tension or energy; being truly excited.
context: Real/genuine tension or energy; being truly excited.

usageExample.en: I was so excited that my real energy came out!

meaning: Reasonable suspicion or a logical doubt.
context: Reasonable suspicion or a logical doubt.
origin: A humorous way to say 'I have a strong, logical reason to suspect this.'

usageExample.kr: 이건 네가 범인이라는 킹리적 갓심이 들어.
usageExample.en: I have a reasonable suspicion that you are the culprit.

meaning: An area near home where you can go wearing slippers (full of convenient facilities).
context: An area near home where you can go wearing slippers (full of convenient facilities).
origin: Inspired by 'Yeok-se-gwon' (station area). Represents convenience of location.

usageExample.kr: 우리 집은 편의점이 바로 앞인 슬세권이야.
usageExample.en: My house is in a 'slipper-zone' with a convenience store right in front.

meaning: Someone who asks others for information instead of searching for it themselves.
context: Someone who asks others for information instead of searching for it themselves.
origin: Also 'Finger Prince'. Used to tease lazy people in online communities.

usageExample.kr: 그건 검색하면 바로 나오는데, 완전 핑공이네.
usageExample.en: You can find that instantly by searching; you're such a finger princess.

meaning: Investing money for one's own mental satisfaction, regardless of the price.
context: Investing money for one's own mental satisfaction, regardless of the price.
origin: Focuses on 'I' (Na) rather than just cost-effectiveness.

usageExample.kr: 나를 위한 선물로 명품 가방 샀어. 나심비 최고!
usageExample.en: I bought a luxury bag as a gift for myself. Peak personal satisfaction!

meaning: Study-Life Balance. The balance between studying and personal life for students.
context: Study-Life Balance. The balance between studying and personal life for students.
origin: A variation of 'Wo-ra-baal' (Work-Life Balance) for the younger generation.

usageExample.en: Study-life balance is important for students these days, too.

meaning: Diary decorating. A popular hobby of using stickers and pens to beautify a planner.
context: Diary decorating. A popular hobby of using stickers and pens to beautify a planner.

usageExample.en: I went shopping for stickers for my diary decorating today.

meaning: Top-loader decorating. Decorating the plastic sleeves used to hold K-pop photocards.
context: Top-loader decorating. Decorating the plastic sleeves used to hold K-pop photocards.

usageExample.kr: 내 최애 포카 넣어두려고 탑꾸 했어.
usageExample.en: I decorated a top-loader to put my bias's photocard in.

meaning: A non-celebrity who is as admirable or talented as a 'God' (God + Ordinary person).
context: A non-celebrity who is as admirable or talented as a 'God' (God + Ordinary person).
origin: Used to describe cool people who aren't famous.

usageExample.kr: 저 사람은 일반인인데 스타일이 완전 갓반인이네.
usageExample.en: That person is a non-celebrity, but their style is totally 'God-ordinary person'.

meaning: A variation of 'Eojjeol-TV' used to further annoy or dismiss someone.
context: A variation of 'Eojjeol-TV' used to further annoy or dismiss someone.
origin: Often followed by many other 'TV-related' words in a rhythmic way.

usageExample.en: Yeah, whatever whatever, I can't hear you~

meaning: The absolute best; the king of kings. A bit classic/old-school but still iconic.
context: The absolute best; the king of kings. A bit classic/old-school but still iconic.

usageExample.kr: 너 오늘 요리 실력 진짜 킹왕짱이다!
usageExample.en: Your cooking skills today are absolutely the best!

meaning: What is this? / What the? (Expressing confusion).
context: What is this? / What the? (Expressing confusion).
origin: Originated from a typo of 'Mwo-im' (What is it?) which became a meme.

usageExample.en: What on earth is this situation?

meaning: A refreshing situation where justice is served or someone says what everyone was thinking.
context: A refreshing situation where justice is served or someone says what everyone was thinking.
origin: The cool, carbonated feeling of a soda metaphorically applied to a social situation.

usageExample.en: The protagonist's revenge was so refreshing [Cider]!

meaning: A frustrating, stuffy situation where things aren't moving forward (opposite of Cider).
context: A frustrating, stuffy situation where things aren't moving forward (opposite of Cider).
origin: The dry, heavy feeling of eating a sweet potato without water.

usageExample.kr: 전개가 너무 느려서 고구마 먹은 것 같아.
usageExample.en: The plot is so slow, it feels like I've eaten a dry sweet potato [frustrating].

meaning: Something that is absurd, nonsensical, or weird but strangely entertaining.
context: Something that is absurd, nonsensical, or weird but strangely entertaining.
origin: Originated from webtoons that had intentionally poor drawings and weird plots.

usageExample.kr: 이 영화 진짜 병맛인데 계속 보게 돼.
usageExample.en: This movie is so absurd, but I can't stop watching it.

meaning: An animated GIF or a short moving image.
context: An animated GIF or a short moving image.

usageExample.kr: 이 귀여운 강아지 움짤 좀 봐!
usageExample.en: Look at this cute puppy GIF!

meaning: An unbeatable wall; something or someone that is so superior you can't even compare.
context: An unbeatable wall; something or someone that is so superior you can't even compare.

usageExample.en: That player's skill is truly an unbeatable wall.

meaning: Being suddenly surprised or shocked.
context: Being suddenly surprised or shocked.

usageExample.en: You appeared so suddenly, you really surprised me!

meaning: Being eliminated or failing instantly and miserably.
context: Being eliminated or failing instantly and miserably.

usageExample.en: I failed the interview instantly.

meaning: The 'God of Shopping' who possesses you and makes you buy things on impulse.
context: The 'God of Shopping' who possesses you and makes you buy things on impulse.
origin: Short for 'Ji-reu-da' (to make a big purchase/throw money).

usageExample.en: The shopping god possessed me, so I finally bought the camera.

meaning: Cleansing one's eyes by looking at something beautiful or handsome.
context: Cleansing one's eyes by looking at something beautiful or handsome.
origin: Metaphor for 'purifying' one's vision after seeing something unpleasant.

usageExample.en: Looking at the beautiful scenery is cleansing my eyes.

meaning: Enjoying a huge feast or showing off delicious food.
context: Enjoying a huge feast or showing off delicious food.

usageExample.kr: 오늘 친구들이랑 제대로 먹부림 하고 왔어.
usageExample.en: I had a real food feast with my friends today.

meaning: Bursting out into sudden, loud laughter.
context: Bursting out into sudden, loud laughter.
origin: Metaphorical sound of laughter exploding like a balloon.

usageExample.en: I totally burst out laughing because of you!

meaning: Clicking the mouse at the speed of light (usually for ticketing or registration).
context: Clicking the mouse at the speed of light (usually for ticketing or registration).

usageExample.en: I'm clicking like crazy to register for classes.

meaning: Unexpected charm. When someone has a hidden trait that surprises you in a good way.
context: Unexpected charm. When someone has a hidden trait that surprises you in a good way.
origin: A common compliment for people with diverse personalities.

usageExample.kr: 겉은 차가워 보이는데 속은 따뜻한 반전매력이 있네.
usageExample.en: You look cold on the outside but have an unexpected warmth.

meaning: Heart-stopping. Used when something is so cute or shocking that your heart 'stops'.
context: Heart-stopping. Used when something is so cute or shocking that your heart 'stops'.

usageExample.en: My heart almost stopped after seeing the puppy photo.

meaning: Extremely fun or funny.
context: Extremely fun or funny.

usageExample.en: This movie is ultra funny!

meaning: Not funny; boring.
context: Not funny; boring.

usageExample.en: That joke is really not funny.

meaning: A great benefit or an unexpected lucky gain.
context: A great benefit or an unexpected lucky gain.
origin: Gae' is used as an intensifier (lit. 'Dog-gain').

usageExample.en: It was a sale period so I got 50% off, what a steal!

meaning: Heart attack. Used when someone is overwhelmingly attractive or cute.
context: Heart attack. Used when someone is overwhelmingly attractive or cute.

usageExample.kr: 아이돌의 윙크 한 번에 심장폭격 당함.
usageExample.en: My heart was attacked by the idol's wink.

meaning: A 'warm' and handsome man who has a kind image.
context: A 'warm' and handsome man who has a kind image.

usageExample.kr: 학교에 새로 온 선생님 진짜 훈남이야.
usageExample.en: The new teacher at school is really a 'warm' handsome guy.

meaning: A 'warm' and beautiful woman with a kind image.
context: A 'warm' and beautiful woman with a kind image.

usageExample.en: Your friend is a really 'warm' beautiful woman.

meaning: Awesome, amazing, or a great success. The most iconic Korean slang.
context: Awesome, amazing, or a great success. The most iconic Korean slang.

usageExample.kr: 와, 이번 시험 다 맞았어? 대박!
usageExample.en: Wow, you got everything right on the exam? Awesome!

meaning: Highly recommended.
context: Highly recommended.

usageExample.en: I highly recommend this restaurant!

meaning: Not recommended.
context: Not recommended.

usageExample.en: That movie is so boring, I don't recommend it.
